mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal deteriorating labor demand that leads equity investors to discount future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. As of mid-2008 PAYEMS has been contracting for six consecutive months, a pattern historically associated with recessionary equity drawdowns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declined from 137640 to 137700 revised value amid multi-month contraction trend into mid-2008
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months with PAYEMS month-over-month declines over a rolling 3-year window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 3% and remains there for at least two consecutive months, the market interprets this as an aggressive easing stance intended to offset a weakening economy, and forward-looking equity valuations rise over a quarterly horizon as discount rates compress. FEDFUNDS stood at 2.0% in June 2008, consistent with this low-rate regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 2.0% as of June 2008 vintage
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following low-rate triggers are negative in more than 50% of historical instances, or the effect reverses sign during periods of concurrent credit stress, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer and construction-sector weakness that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the following month. HOUST registered 973 thousand units (revised) as of May 2008, below this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 973.0 in May 2008, below 1000k threshold
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns exceed the unconditional baseline positive-day rate (65.5%) during HOUST sub-1000 threshold periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Short-run momentum in industrial production (INDPRO), measured as the sign of its most recent month-over-month change, is positively correlated with near-term S&P 500 returns because production growth reflects real-time corporate output strength ahead of earnings releases. This is tested at a 5-day horizon to capture immediate market reaction to industrial data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 110.9051 for May 2008 subsequently revised down to 100.2674, reflecting high revision risk
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month sign flips negative or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) is rising while the Federal Reserve is simultaneously cutting rates (FEDFUNDS declining), the market prices in an anticipatory recovery driven by monetary stimulus, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon once the combined signal of labor deterioration and policy easing is present, reflecting a policy-put dynamic. UNRATE rose to 5.6% (revised) while FEDFUNDS sat at 2.0% in mid-2008, indicating this joint condition.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising 3-month trend AND FEDFUNDS declining 3-month trend
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised to 5.6% June 2008 amid FEDFUNDS at 2.0%
failure_condition: If joint UNRATE-rising/FEDFUNDS-falling episodes produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the additive single-series effects (p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary regime where CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating year-over-year above 4% while growth indicators are simultaneously weak, rising inflation compresses equity valuations via higher expected discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month. CPIAUCSL rose to 215.208 (revised) reflecting elevated inflation pressure from oil price shocks in mid-2008.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 215.208 for May 2008 vintage, consistent with elevated YoY inflation
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns during CPI-above-4%-YoY triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a compounding collapse in aggregate consumer demand that is not captured by either series alone, and this joint weakness predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings expectations for consumer-facing sectors deteriorate. RSAFS revised value fell to 368,926 million from a vintage of 384,211 million alongside continued PAYEMS softness in mid-2008.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised down to 368926 from vintage 384211 in June 2008 amid continued payroll losses
failure_condition: If joint RSAFS/PAYEMS decline episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over the single-series models (delta Rank IC < 0.01), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime identified by two or more consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth (GDPC1), equity markets exhibit a delayed but positive forward reaction over a quarterly horizon once the trough in demand approaches, as markets anticipate cyclical recovery ahead of confirmed data. This mechanism activates specifically when GDPC1 growth has been negative for multiple quarters, a condition plausible given the deteriorating 2008 macro backdrop.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth negative for 2+ consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value 11703.6 billion for 2008Q1 reflecting weak growth prior to major downward revision to 16843.003 under later benchmark
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following confirmed multi-quarter GDPC1 contraction triggers are negative in more than 50% of historical instances, or the effect is indistinguishable from random walk baseline (Rank IC < 0.02), the hypothesis is rejected.
